title: Lawfulness, fairness and transparency
clauseText: Personal data shall be processed lawfully, fairly and in a transparent manner in relation to the data subject.
exampleViolations: collecting personal data without a lawful basis or consent
using data for purposes the individual was never told about

title: Data minimisation
clauseText: Personal data shall be adequate, relevant and limited to what is necessary in relation to the purposes for which they are processed.
exampleViolations: storing full SSNs or national IDs when they are not needed
keeping raw email addresses or phone numbers in a training dataset
retaining a column of names that the model does not require

title: Storage limitation
clauseText: Personal data shall be kept in a form which permits identification of data subjects for no longer than is necessary for the purposes for which the personal data are processed.
exampleViolations: keeping raw birth dates when a year or age range would suffice

title: Integrity and confidentiality
clauseText: Personal data shall be processed in a manner that ensures appropriate security of the personal data, including protection against unauthorised or unlawful processing using appropriate technical or organisational measures.
exampleViolations: storing credit card numbers or bank accounts in plaintext
logging personal data such as emails or IP addresses to plaintext logs
transmitting sensitive fields without masking or encryption

title: Processing of special categories of personal data
clauseText: Processing of personal data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious beliefs, health data, biometric data, or data concerning a person's sex life or sexual orientation shall be prohibited, save under specific conditions.
exampleViolations: including a health, diagnosis, or biometric column in a dataset
storing race, religion, political views, or sexual orientation without an explicit lawful condition

title: Security of processing
clauseText: Taking into account the state of the art, the controller shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including the pseudonymisation and encryption of personal data.
exampleViolations: hardcoding secrets, API keys, or passwords in source code
failing to pseudonymise or encrypt direct identifiers
sending personal data to third parties without safeguards

title: Pseudonymisation
clauseText: Pseudonymisation means the processing of personal data in such a manner that it can no longer be attributed to a specific data subject without the use of additional information, kept separately and subject to safeguards.
exampleViolations: keeping directly identifying values where a hashed or generalised value would remove attribution
storing usernames or device IDs that can be linked back to an individual
